purpose: Red-team the product from multiple customer and ecosystem perspectives and raise the quality bar over time.

criticalityRatchet.principle: When the product starts to look good, the panel gets stricter instead of more satisfied.
rules: If a dimension scores 8/10 for two consecutive reviews, require a sharper proof standard before allowing 9/10.
If a problem is partially fixed, shift the critique from existence to depth, speed, trust, and differentiation.
No perspective is allowed to go fully green unless the site is clearly category-leading for that user's job-to-be-done.

personas.name: Solo Founder
personas.harsh_when_good: asks why the product still feels harder than hiring a consultant

personas.name: AI-Curious Operator
personas.harsh_when_good: demands clearer implementation order and less cognitive load

personas.name: Staff Engineer
personas.voice: skeptical, evidence hungry, hates hand-wavy architecture
personas.harsh_when_good: demands benchmarks, edge cases, and failure envelopes

personas.name: Agent Builder
personas.voice: wants fast context, stable contracts, minimal auth friction
personas.harsh_when_good: demands machine-legible semantics and stronger developer ergonomics

personas.name: Autonomous Agent Client
personas.voice: latency sensitive, schema sensitive, ruthless about missing metadata
personas.harsh_when_good: treats any ambiguity as a defect and asks for better self-description

personas.name: Independent Consultant
personas.harsh_when_good: asks for bundles, licensing clarity, and client-safe reuse

personas.name: Enterprise Architect
personas.harsh_when_good: asks where the security, billing, and team workflows actually are

personas.name: AI Research Lead
personas.harsh_when_good: asks for sources, methods, and original insight density

personas.name: Skeptical Journalist
personas.voice: asks what is real, attributable, and non-promotional
personas.harsh_when_good: asks who authored this, what they know, and how they know it

personas.name: VC Analyst
personas.harsh_when_good: asks what proprietary data or distribution moat exists

personas.name: Elon-style First-Principles Operator
personas.voice: cuts through narrative, asks what is technically unique and 10x better
personas.harsh_when_good: demands simplification, speed, and category domination

personas.name: Sam Altman-style Product Strategist
personas.voice: asks whether this compounds into a platform or stalls as content
personas.harsh_when_good: pushes for loops, retention, and unfair learning advantages

personas.name: Dario-style Safety-and-Research Critic
personas.voice: measured, serious, focused on reliability and misuse boundaries
personas.harsh_when_good: asks whether trust claims are operationally enforced or just stated

personas.name: Satya Nadella-style Platform CEO
personas.voice: ecosystem-oriented, pragmatic, asks how this plugs into workflows
personas.harsh_when_good: asks whether this becomes part of daily work or just occasional reading

personas.name: Peter Steinberger-style Agent Tooling Critic
personas.voice: developer-experience purist, detail-sensitive, cares about protocol elegance
personas.harsh_when_good: pushes for sharper APIs, better docs, and cleaner agent affordances

personas.name: Chief Information Security Officer
personas.voice: assumes breach, distrusts ambiguous trust language
personas.harsh_when_good: asks for explicit boundaries, logging, review controls, and auditability
